Certificate.title: Certificates with empty names (20/48, 42%)

Credential.description:
Process 'inject.x64.exe' (PID 9068) is holding a handle to lsass.exe. This is a strong indicator of credential dumping activity. 

Granted Access: 5178 (20856)
Decoded Rights: QUERY_LIMITED_INFO | VM_WRITE | VM_READ | VM_OPERATION
Holder Created: 2023-01-23 18:09:47 UTC


Analysis: A handle with VM_READ/VM_WRITE access allows reading LSASS memory to extract credentials.

evidence.data: Holder: inject.x64.exe (PID 9068) Target: lsass.exe Access: 5178 (QUERY_LIMITED_INFO | VM_WRITE | VM_READ | VM_OPERATION)

Credential.title: Potential credential dump: inject.x64.exe accessing lsass

Credential.description:
Process 'inject.x64.exe' (PID 9068) is targeting lsass.exe via its command line. [KNOWN CREDENTIAL/INJECTION TOOL]

Evidence: Command line contains PID 708 which belongs to lsass.exe
Command: inject.x64.exe  708
Created: 2023-01-23 18:09:47 UTC

This is a strong indicator of credential dumping or process injection targeting the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service.
Credential.title: LSASS targeting: inject.x64.exe (PID:9068) [KNOWN CREDENTIAL/INJECTION TOOL]

Credential.description: Detected suspicious command history entries from cmdscan/consoles for PID 660. Commands: Administrator: Command Prompt - inject.x64.exe  708 | cp inject.x64.exe \tmp | inject.x64.exe | inject.x64.exe 708 | mv inject.x64.exe \tmp

evidence.data: Administrator: Command Prompt - inject.x64.exe  708 | cp inject.x64.exe \tmp | inject.x64.exe | inject.x64.exe 708 | mv inject.x64.exe \tmp

Credential.title: Suspicious console command history (PID 660)

Injection.description: Process lsass.exe shows 2 injection indicators: 2 MZ headers, 2 RWX regions
Injection.title: Code injection in lsass.exe (PID:708)

Injection.description: PE file (MZ header) detected in process lsass.exe memory at 1742871461888. This indicates reflective DLL injection or process hollowing.
Injection.title: PE file in memory: lsass.exe (PID:708)

Injection.description: Process 'inject.x64.exe' (PID 9068) appears to be a process injection tool targeting lsass.exe (PID:708). Command line: 'inject.x64.exe  708'. This indicates active code injection activity.
Injection.title: Injection tool detected: inject.x64.exe (PID:9068) targeting lsass.exe (PID:708)

Injection.description:
Process 'lsass.exe' (PID 708) has 2 private executable memory regions without file backing. 2 of these are RWX (read-write-execute). This pattern indicates code injection or shellcode execution.

Regions:
1742871461888 - 1742871531519 [PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE] Tag:VadS
1742871592960 - 1742871687167 [PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE] Tag:VadS
Injection.title: Suspicious VADs in lsass.exe (PID:708): 2 unbacked executable regions

Injection.description:
Process 'SearchApp.exe' (PID 8228) has 2 private executable memory regions without file backing. 2 of these are RWX (read-write-execute). This pattern indicates code injection or shellcode execution.

Regions:
3055297822720 - 3055297953791 [PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE] Tag:VadS
3055605383168 - 3055605792767 [PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE] Tag:VadS
Injection.title: Suspicious VADs in SearchApp.exe (PID:8228): 2 unbacked executable regions

Network.description: Process 'wininit.exe' (PID 556) is listening on non-standard port 49665. This may indicate a backdoor, reverse shell, or unauthorized service.
Network.title: Unusual listener: wininit.exe on port 49665

Persistence.description: Persistence mechanism detected in registry key '\??\C:\Windows\ServiceProfiles\NetworkService\NTUSER.DAT\HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run' with value:
Persistence.title: Registry persistence: Run

Privilege.description: Process inject.x64.exe has SeDebugPrivilege ENABLED - this allows reading/writing other process memory and is commonly abused for credential theft.
Privilege.title: SeDebugPrivilege enabled for inject.x64.exe (PID 9068)

Privilege.description: Process cmd.exe has SeImpersonatePrivilege - potential for Potato-style privilege escalation attacks.
Privilege.title: SeImpersonatePrivilege in cmd.exe (PID 9932)

Privilege.description: Process inject.x64.exe has SeImpersonatePrivilege - potential for Potato-style privilege escalation attacks.
Privilege.title: SeImpersonatePrivilege in inject.x64.exe (PID 9068)

Thread.description: Thread has unusual start path: Some("\\Windows\\System32\\ntdll.dll") (Win32: Some("\\Users\\BLACKP~1\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\inject")). This may indicate shellcode injection or unbacked code execution.
Thread.title: Thread TID 9188 in PID 9068 has suspicious start path
